storyContext.name: admin-delete-menu-item
storyContext.problem: Admins need a way to hard-delete obsolete menu items while preserving order history. Current admin surface (Story 1.5 add, Story 1.2.2 availability toggle) offers no removal. Because Order.menuItemId is a FK to MenuItem.id, an unconditional delete would either break referential integrity or destroy history — neither is acceptable.

policy.rule: Hard delete permitted only when zero Orders reference the MenuItem.
Any referencing Order (any OrderStatus, including CANCELLED) blocks the delete with 409.
Admin auth uses inline X-Admin header check — no filter / no @PreAuthorize.
No DTOs — entity = API, per v1 tech-spec decision.

ubiquitousLanguage.term: Entity in ch.elca.training.lunch.menu (id, name, priceChf, available).
Entity in ch.elca.training.lunch.order referencing MenuItem via menuItemId (FK).
HTTP request carrying header X-Admin: true.
Order row whose menuItemId equals target MenuItem.id, any OrderStatus.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Existing item + zero referencing orders + admin request => 204 No Content, empty body, subsequent GET /api/v1/menu/{id} returns 404.
Unknown id + admin request => 404 with body {code:MENU_ITEM_NOT_FOUND, message:non-empty}. Reuse existing MenuItemNotFoundException + GlobalExceptionHandler mapping (from Story 1.7).
Existing item + at least one referencing order (any OrderStatus) + admin request => 409 with body {code:MENU_ITEM_HAS_ORDERS, message:non-empty}. Item NOT deleted.
X-Admin header absent OR value != "true" => 403 with same error shape used by POST /api/v1/menu/items (Story 1.5).

file.reason: Pattern for inline X-Admin header check on addItem endpoint
Add new @ExceptionHandler for MenuItemHasOrdersException; existing NotFound mapping stays
Add derived query: boolean existsByMenuItemId(UUID menuItemId)
Thrown by MenuService.deleteById when referencing orders exist
file.messageTemplate: Menu item %s cannot be deleted because orders reference it

implementationOutline.step: Add MenuItemHasOrdersException(UUID id) mirroring MenuItemNotFoundException's shape.
Add existsByMenuItemId(UUID) derived query on OrderRepository.
Add @DeleteMapping("/items/{id}") on MenuController — inline X-Admin check matching addItem.
Add @ExceptionHandler(MenuItemHasOrdersException.class) in GlobalExceptionHandler returning HTTP 409 + ApiError("MENU_ITEM_HAS_ORDERS", ex.getMessage()).

test.strategy: add cases to existing test class OR create MenuControllerDeleteTest
test.case: Happy path — 204, item removed from DB
409 with referencing order — asserts $.code, item still present
deleteById calls menuRepository.delete when no orders
deleteById throws MenuItemNotFoundException for unknown id
existsByMenuItemId returns true when at least one Order exists (any status)
existsByMenuItemId returns false when no Order references the id

definitionOfDone.item: Controller, service, repository tests cover the four branches
Manual smoke: .\mvnw spring-boot:run + curl DELETE returns 204 for a seeded item
No SonarQube S1192 / S2699 violations in new code

rule.note: Duplicate string literals — extract error codes/messages to constants if reused
Every new test must contain at least one assertion
Never catch Exception/Throwable — throw and let GlobalExceptionHandler map

deviationsFromELCAi.deviation: No Jira ticket / no Analyzed transition — workshop repo does not use Jira.
No sprint-status.yaml bootstrap — orphan artefact without a board.
No tech-spec-epic-1.md — flat docs/tech-spec.md covers all epics.
Base branch is main, not develop — workshop is trunk-based.
